### Post by triangleSLO on 2009-07-04
I tried ubuntu nvidia drivers version 173,180 and official nvidia driver  185.18.14 but screen is still flickering. 
My graphics card is GeForce 6600.

```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder63)  Wed May 27 03:15:36 PDT 2009

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier     "Monitor0"
	VendorName     "Unknown"
	ModelName      "Unknown"
	HorizSync       28.0 - 33.0
	VertRefresh     43.0 - 72.0
	Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier     "Screen0"
	Device         "Device0"
	Monitor        "Monitor0"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth       24
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"glx"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier     "Mouse0"
	Driver         "mouse"
	Option         "Protocol" "auto"
	Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
	Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier     "Keyboard0"
	Driver         "kbd"
	# generated from default
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Layout0"
	Screen      0  "Screen0"
	InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
	InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier     "Device0"
	VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
	Driver	"nvidia"
	Option	"NoLogo"	"True"
EndSection

```

Please help my head is starting to hurt because of damn flickering.

### Post by markharding557 on 2009-07-04
does this occur using the open source driver?
if not then we know it's something with the nvidia driver.

### Post by triangleSLO on 2009-07-04
It happens with both drivers.
It also takes some time (30 seconds) before gui starts. Monitor is turning on and off like driver is searching for right resolution or something.

---

### Post by merlinus on 2009-07-04
Thie problem may be in the monitor section of xorg.conf.  Here is mine:

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "Hitachi CM751"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 94.0
    VertRefresh     50.0 - 160.0
EndSection

Look at the huge difference in HorizSync and VertRefresh compared with yours.

---

I'll change those settings.

### Post by merlinus on 2009-07-04
Unfortunately you cannot cheat by increasing the specs beyond what the monitor can do.  What are the options in System/Preferences/Display?

### Post by merlinus on 2009-07-04
```
gksu nvidia-settings
```
